title: centaurea × cervantesi
intro: Centaurea × cervantesi, also known as the Cervantes' cornflower or false cornflower, is a perennial plant in the aster family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The health benefits of Centaurea × cervantesi include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Centaurea × cervantesi can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and infections.
